comment_content:
Just finished this series. Normally I don't really enjoy detective movies or shows; I always feel the detective too overpowered, always knowing everything, and somehow always right in the end without explaining what they saw or thought.

This one is different. I LOVE IT. Every single character in this teams have their own important role, shared thoughts and explained what they found. Akram is really a lovable character. I love him immediately. Eventhought it starts a little bit slow, trust me, it's worth the wait. The intensity grows as more secrets are disclosed piece by piece.

REALLY HOPE NETFLIX WON'T CANCEL THIS ONE FOR ANY REASON. It has a lot of things to discover, even I want to know about Akram's past. I don't mind if Netflix wants to make a special episode just for Akram.

comment_content:
Dept. Q – One of the Best Series I’ve Ever Watched

I have never written a review before. I usually just watch and move on. But Dept. Q left me no choice. I had to say something.

From the first episode, I was completely drawn in. I didn’t plan to binge it, but once I started, I couldn’t stop. Episode after episode, I was glued to the screen. It grabbed me in a way that very few shows ever have. I didn’t just watch it, I felt it. Every moment. Every scene.

What hit me most was how emotional the story became without ever trying too hard. It was subtle, honest, and deeply moving. By the time I reached the final episode, I found myself tearing up. That rarely happens. It wasn’t just sad, it was something deeper. Something real.

The suspense was handled perfectly. It kept me on edge, fully invested. But it wasn’t just about solving cases. It was about people, pain, justice, regret, and hope. There was a weight to everything, and it stayed with me.

The acting was outstanding. I usually notice when performances feel off, but every actor in this series was believable. Not a single moment felt forced. You could feel the emotion in their eyes, in the silence between lines. It was raw and powerful.

I know everyone sees things differently, and some might find small flaws. But for me, I wouldn’t change a thing. Everything felt intentional, and it worked. The writing, the pacing, the tone, the atmosphere. It all came together in a way that was rare and unforgettable.

Dept. Q is not just a good show. It’s something more. It reminded me why I love storytelling. Not just for the entertainment, but for how it makes you feel something real. It pulled me in completely, and even after it ended, I kept thinking about it.

If you haven’t seen it yet, do yourself a favor and watch it. Let it take you on that journey. It might just stay with you the way it stayed with me.
reply_content: I couldn't of said it better. Spot on it had me hooked from the beginning and I binged it in 1 day. Brilliant

From the first episode, I was completely drawn in. I didn’t plan to binge it, but once I started, I couldn’t stop. Episode after episode, I was glued to the screen. It grabbed me in a way that very few shows ever have. I didn’t just watch it, I felt it. Every moment. Every scene.

What hit me most was how emotional the story became without ever trying too hard. It was subtle, honest, and deeply moving. By the time I reached the final episode, I found myself tearing up. That rarely happens. It wasn’t just sad, it was something deeper. Something real.

The suspense was handled perfectly. It kept me on edge, fully invested. But it wasn’t just about solving cases. It was about people, pain, justice, regret, and hope. There was a weight to everything, and it stayed with me.

The acting was outstanding. I usually notice when performances feel off, but every actor in this series was believable. Not a single moment felt forced. You could feel the emotion in their eyes, in the silence between lines. It was raw and powerful.

I know everyone sees things differently, and some might find small flaws. But for me, I wouldn’t change a thing. Everything felt intentional, and it worked. The writing, the pacing, the tone, the atmosphere. It all came together in a way that was rare and unforgettable.

Dept. Q is not just a good show. It’s something more. It reminded me why I love storytelling. Not just for the entertainment, but for how it makes you feel something real. It pulled me in completely, and even after it ended, I kept thinking about it.

If you haven’t seen it yet, do yourself a favor and watch it. Let it take you on that journey. It might just stay with you the way it stayed with me.
